title: The entry plug placement and the gravity
display_title: The entry plug placement and the gravity
wikitext:
[]: The entry plug placement and the gravity             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 18, 2008 4:59 pm Well, We know that the entry plug goes inside the Eva's neck at some angle so while the Eva is standing the pilot inside is sitting in vertical position, facing the horizon. The connection seems to be stiff so does that mean that during the fight with Ramiel when Eva was lying, Shinji was facing the ground, being upside down? It must be a helluva headache hanging upside down and seeing everything on screens turned the right way. Or maybe there's place for the entry plug to shift and change it's position?

[]: Re: The entry plug placement and the gravity             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Aug 18, 2008 6:14 pm simon wrote: Or maybe there's place for the entry plug to shift and change it's position?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 18, 2008 7:56 pm Yeah pretty much as above. Ex: Asuka fighting the Harpies. Her hair and everything.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 18, 2008 9:33 pm I'm fairly certain that the chair is tilted at the appropriate angle so you're "level" even when the tube is in the Eva

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Aug 18, 2008 10:30 pm V wrote: I'm fairly certain that the chair is tilted at the appropriate angle so you're "level" even when the tube is in the Eva

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:16 pm Gamer_2k4 wrote: V wrote: I'm fairly certain that the chair is tilted at the appropriate angle so you're "level" even when the tube is in the Eva

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Tue Aug 19, 2008 4:48 am Action_Bastard wrote: Gamer_2k4 wrote: V wrote: I'm fairly certain that the chair is tilted at the appropriate angle so you're "level" even when the tube is in the Eva

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Aug 19, 2008 3:38 pm "LCL is a magic wand" remember, LCL will cushion their motion/posture, whatever position they are in; further, remember that they are pretty much strapped into the seat in such cases.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Aug 19, 2008 6:25 pm V wrote: "LCL is a magic wand" remember, LCL will cushion their motion/posture, whatever position they are in; further, remember that they are pretty much strapped into the seat in such cases.

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Tue Aug 19, 2008 7:51 pm Action_Bastard wrote: But when it comes to them being strapped in, I know Shinji is, but the females seem to be just sitting in them, not strapped down by anything.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 19, 2008 7:59 pm Have you considered that perhaps the seat 'bobsled' part can rotate around the cylinder to adjust for such issues?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Tue Aug 19, 2008 8:09 pm They have, and the conclusion is that that does diddly squat for most orientations. For one, directly upsidown and rotated, and now you are on a slope with your head at the bottom. If the Eva is lying face down, then its no difference at all. Faceup on its back, and there's no difference at all. See where this is going? It would be a stupid and wasteful function that does effectively nothing most of the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Tue Aug 19, 2008 8:10 pm Surely the plug is too narrow to allow for rotation of more than 30 degrees or so? I could be wrong, but I'm sure the bob-sled bit is way longer than the plug is wide, meaning that although that idea could help with shock-absorbance/minor rotation, I can't see it helping if your Eva is doing a headstand... EDIT: Ignore me. I get what you mean now. Please accept the apologies of a tired idiot!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 19, 2008 8:25 pm Formless One wrote: They have, and the conclusion is that that does diddly squat for most orientations. For one, directly upsidown and rotated, and now you are on a slope with your head at the bottom. If the Eva is lying face down, then its no difference at all. Faceup on its back, and there's no difference at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Sep 02, 2008 2:40 pm this may be one of those times where they didnt feel like putting too much thought into making something realistic guys the fact that the plug should go into the spine already makes no sense orientation wise because that would mean the pilot is normally facing straight down

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Sep 02, 2008 2:54 pm ^ WAT!?!

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Sep 02, 2008 4:02 pm what to what?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Sep 02, 2008 5:45 pm Axell wrote: the fact that the plug should go into the spine already makes no sense orientation wise because that would mean the pilot is normally facing straight down

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Tue Sep 02, 2008 7:23 pm I took some screen caps of the plug being inserted into the Eva, but couldn't upload them, they were too big. Anyway, I think Axell is right. I mean, the plug was already facing downwards, except on a slight angle so it wouldn't face straight down. And because the pilot from the start was already sitting in the downwards position, whenever he/she would forward, it wouldn't be eye-level to the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Gydafud » Tue Sep 02, 2008 11:14 pm Couldn't the plug itself "spin" within the entry.. slot. So if the EVA is lying down on its front, inside you would be facing the ground. But if you roll the plug inside the EVA, you would be upright again? I am confusing myself here...
summary: []: The entry plug placement and the gravity             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 18, 2008 4:59 pm Well, We know that the entry plug goes inside the Eva's neck at some angle so while the Eva is standing the pilot inside is sitting in vertical position, facing the horizon. The connection seems to be stiff so does that mean that during the fight with Ramiel when Eva was lying, Shinji was facing the ground, being upside down? It must
